verses.fhb_text: For this reason I, Paul, the prisoner of Christ Jesus on behalf of you Gentiles—
verses.decision_notes: Pauline opening; "Christ Jesus" preserved per Greek order (FHB-1.2-20).

verses.fhb_text: assuming you have heard of the stewardship of our Father's grace that was given to me for you,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Theos distinguished from Christ as source of apostolic grace.

Renderings:
• "our Father's grace" — Theos paired with grace given for the Gentile mission; Father distinguished from Christ Jesus (v.1) per FHB-1.2-1 NT X-of-God general clause

Hebrew/Greek: tēs charitos tou theou (the grace of God) → "our Father's grace"

verses.fhb_text: how the mystery was made known to me by revelation, as I have briefly written above.
verses.decision_notes: Narrative flow; no FHB-distinctive choices.

verses.fhb_text: When you read this, you can perceive my insight into the mystery of Christ,
verses.decision_notes: Standard narrative.

verses.fhb_text: which in past generations was not made known to humanity as it has now been revealed by the Spirit to his holy apostles and prophets—
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Calque avoidance + Spirit Resolution.

Renderings:
• "to humanity" — Greek "tois huiois tōn anthrōpōn" (sons of men) is a Semitic idiom; modernized per FHB-7.12-1 calque avoidance
• "by the Spirit" — bare pneumati in post-Pentecost apostolic-revelation context; the Holy Spirit is the antecedent per FHB-1.2-7 (D)

verses.fhb_text: namely, that the Gentiles are fellow heirs, members of the same body, and partakers together of the promise in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
verses.decision_notes: The mystery's content; "Christ Jesus" preserved per Pauline order.

verses.fhb_text: Of this gospel I became a servant according to the gift of our Father's grace that was given to me by the working of his power.
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "our Father's grace" — Theos as source of grace, distinguished from Christ; per NT X-of-God (FHB-1.2-1)
• "his power" — antecedent is the Father; preserve possessive

Hebrew/Greek: tēs charitos tou theou → "our Father's grace"

verses.fhb_text: To me, though I am the very least of all the saints, this grace was given: to preach to the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ,
verses.decision_notes: Paul's humility-statement; standard rendering.

verses.fhb_text: and to bring to light for everyone what is the stewardship of the mystery hidden for ages in our Father who created all things,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father named as creator — Genesis 1 anchor in Pauline frame.

Renderings:
• "in our Father who created all things" — Theos as creator-source distinguished from Christ (v.11 "in Christ Jesus our Lord"); Genesis 1 anchor + NT X-of-God per FHB-1.2-1

Hebrew/Greek: en tō theō tō ta panta ktisanti (in God who created all things) → "in our Father who created all things"

verses.fhb_text: so that through the church the manifold wisdom of our Father might now be made known to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places.
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "the manifold wisdom of our Father" — Theos distinguished from Christ in the eternal-purpose context (v.11); NT X-of-God general clause

Hebrew/Greek: hē polypoikilos sophia tou theou → "the manifold wisdom of our Father"

verses.fhb_text: This was according to the eternal purpose that he accomplished in Christ Jesus our Lord,
verses.decision_notes: Father's eternal purpose accomplished in Jesus; "Christ Jesus our Lord" preserved per Pauline confessional form.

verses.fhb_text: in whom we have boldness and confident access through faith in him.
verses.decision_notes: Access through Jesus; antecedent "him" = Jesus from v.11.

verses.fhb_text: So I ask you not to lose heart over what I am suffering for you, which is your glory.
verses.decision_notes: Transitional bridge to the prayer.

verses.fhb_text: For this reason I bow my knees before the Father,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Explicit Father naming — Paul addresses our Father in prayer.

Renderings:
• "the Father" — Paul addresses the Father in the prayer; preserve as written (the chapter's explicit filial anchor)

Hebrew/Greek: pros ton patera → "before the Father"

verses.fhb_text: from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father as the source of all family identity — Greek wordplay pater/patria invisible in English.

Renderings:
• "every family in heaven and on earth is named" — preserve Paul's universal claim; the antecedent "from whom" is the Father of v.14

Hebrew/Greek: pasa patria... onomazetai — patria (family/lineage) shares the root of patēr (Father); every family bears the Father's name. Footnote earns its place (FHB-distinctive — the wordplay is invisible in English).
verses.footnote: Every family in heaven and on earth takes its name from our Father — he is the source of all fatherhood, and no family identity exists apart from his.

verses.fhb_text: that according to the riches of his glory he may grant you to be strengthened with power through his Spirit in your inner being,
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "his Spirit" — possessive antecedent is the Father (v.14); per FHB-1.2-7 (A) preserve speaker's naming without padding to "the Holy Spirit"

verses.fhb_text: so that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith—that you, being rooted and grounded in love,
verses.decision_notes: Em dash earns its place — a true parenthetical pivot from indwelling Christ to the love-rooted result; would be weakened by comma.

verses.fhb_text: may have strength to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth,
verses.decision_notes: Fourfold parallel preserved per FHB-9.4-2 (intentional parallelism).

verses.fhb_text: and to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of our Father.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Christ's love and the Father's fullness — both Persons named in the prayer's climax.

Renderings:
• "the fullness of our Father" — Theos distinguished from Christ in the same clause; NT X-of-God per FHB-1.2-1

Hebrew/Greek: pan to plērōma tou theou (all the fullness of God) → "all the fullness of our Father"

verses.fhb_text: Now to him who is able to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think, according to the power at work within us—
verses.decision_notes: Doxology opening; em dash carries the suspension into the glory-statement of v.21 (genuine dramatic pause).

verses.fhb_text: to him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus throughout all generations, forever and ever. Amen.
verses.decision_notes: Doxology closing; "Amen" preserved per FHB-1.2-26 (loanwords). "Christ Jesus" per Pauline order.

chapter_notes: Ephesians 3 is one of the NT's most explicitly filial chapters — Paul names the Father directly at v.14 and identifies him as the source of all family (v.15). Father-rendering of Theos applies throughout (vv.2, 7, 9, 10, 19) per FHB-1.2-1 NT X-of-God general clause: Theos is consistently distinguished from Christ in this chapter's argument (grace given/Christ preached; Father created/Christ accomplished; love of Christ/fullness of Father). The patria/pater wordplay at vv.14-15 carries the chapter's pastoral weight — the Father isn't just one father among many; he is the source from whom every family takes its name. One new footnote category introduced: every_family_from_father.
